title: A European Rover's Journey to Mars
summary: A European rover named Rosalind Franklin is finally getting a ride to Mars after many delays and changes. It was first planned to launch in 2009 with Russia's help, but that didn't happen. Then, NASA agreed to help, but they backed out in 2012 because of money problems. Europe then worked with Russia again, but when Russia invaded Ukraine in 2022, Europe stopped the partnership. Now, NASA is stepping in to launch the rover on a SpaceX Falcon Heavy rocket, maybe in 2028. The rover will look for signs of life on Mars by studying the soil. This story shows how space missions can face big challenges but keep going with help from different countries.

questions.question: Which country's space agency is helping Europe launch the rover now?

background_read: Mars is a planet in our solar system that scientists are very interested in because it might have had life long ago. Rovers are robots sent to explore Mars, like NASA's Curiosity and Perseverance. The European Space Agency (ESA) is Europe's group for space missions, and they have been trying to send their own rover for many years. This story shows how space projects often need help from different countries and can face delays due to money, politics, or other problems, but they keep trying to achieve big goals like finding life on other planets.
Article_Structure: WHO: The European Space Agency (ESA) and NASA are working together on the Rosalind Franklin rover mission.
WHAT: A European rover is being sent to Mars to look for signs of life, after many delays and changes in plans.
WHERE: The rover will launch from Kennedy Space Center in Florida and land on Mars.
WHY: To explore Mars and search for evidence of past or present life, despite challenges like broken partnerships and political issues.

perspectives.description: They are excited to finally send their rover to Mars after years of waiting and working with different partners.
They see this as a chance to help an international partner and advance Mars exploration together.
They lost the opportunity to send their instruments to Mars when the partnership ended due to the Ukraine invasion.
